join_order.note: Load prebuilt hash_ps_partkey, iterate green part keys, collect matching partsupp rows; filter ps_suppkey into a hash_set for lineitem probe

join_order.build: partsupp_result: compact_hash_map keyed by (l_partkey, l_suppkey) → ps_supplycost
join_order.note: Scan lineitem sequentially; probe (l_partkey, l_suppkey) composite key into partsupp_map. Emit matched rows.

join_order.build: lineitem_result: compact_hash_map keyed by l_orderkey → (amount_numerator, l_suppkey)
join_order.note: Scan orders sequentially; probe l_orderkey → extract year from o_orderdate via O(1) lookup table
supplier is 100K rows with dense suppkey 1–100K; use flat array for O(1) zero-miss lookup
25-entry flat array; O(1) lookup with near-zero cost

logical_plan.aggregation_note: GROUP BY (nation, o_year): 25 nations × 7 years (1992–1998) = 175 groups max. Use flat array[25][7] indexed by (nation_idx, year-1992) for O(1) updates with no hash overhead.

filter_pushdown.output: bitset or flat vector of matching p_partkey values (~108K keys)
filter_pushdown.note: p_name is raw string (not dictionary encoded); scan all 2M rows, check strstr(name, 'green') != nullptr. Late-materialize: only load p_name for filtering, output only p_partkey.

late_materialization.part: Load p_partkey and p_name only; p_name used for filter, p_partkey for downstream joins. No other part columns needed.
late_materialization.nation: n_name loaded upfront into flat array (25 entries, negligible cost)
late_materialization.lineitem: Load only l_partkey, l_suppkey, l_orderkey, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_quantity — skip l_shipdate, l_tax, l_returnflag, l_linestatus (not needed by Q9)

data_structures.part_filter: flat_vector<int32_t> of green p_partkey values (~108K entries); also build bitset(2097152) for O(1) membership test during partsupp probe
data_structures.partsupp_join: prebuilt_index_load(hash_ps_partkey) — load indexes/hash_ps_partkey.bin via mmap; iterate green part keys, collect (ps_partkey, ps_suppkey, ps_supplycost) rows into compact_vector; then build compact_hash_map<uint64_t,(ps_supplycost)> keyed by composite key (ps_partkey<<32|ps_suppkey) for lineitem probe
data_structures.lineitem_join_map: compact_hash_map<uint64_t, ps_supplycost_int64> keyed by (l_partkey<<32|l_suppkey), pre-sized to ~430K entries at 75% load factor (power-of-2 bucket count = 524288+)
data_structures.orders_join_map: compact_hash_map<int32_t, tuple(l_suppkey,amount_partial)> keyed by l_orderkey, pre-sized to ~178K entries (power-of-2 = 262144)
data_structures.supplier_lookup: flat_array<int8_t>[100001] indexed by s_suppkey → s_nationkey; O(1) cache-friendly lookup, ~100KB fits in L2 cache
data_structures.nation_lookup: flat_array<string>[25] indexed by n_nationkey → n_name; 25 entries, trivially cache-resident
data_structures.aggregation: flat_array<int64_t>[25][10] indexed by [nation_idx][o_year - 1992] → sum_profit (scaled int64); ~250 entries, fits in single cache line region

phases.note: Parallel scan of part/p_name.bin and part/p_partkey.bin across 64 threads, morsel size ~32K rows. Thread-local vectors of green p_partkeys, merged after. Then build shared bitset (atomic bit-set ops or merge at end).

Partition green p_partkey vector across 64 threads; each thread looks up its partition in prebuilt hash_ps_partkey index. Thread-local result vectors merged into shared compact_hash_map (ps_partkey,ps_suppkey)->ps_supplycost.
Parallel scan of lineitem (59.9M rows) across 64 threads, morsel size ~64K rows. Each thread probes shared read-only (ps_partkey,ps_suppkey) map. Thread-local result vectors for matched rows.
Parallel scan of orders (15M rows) across 64 threads. Each thread probes shared read-only l_orderkey map. Compute YEAR from o_orderdate via O(1) YEAR_TABLE lookup.

Each thread maintains a local flat_array[25][10] for aggregation. After supplier/nation lookups (O(1) array accesses), accumulate sum_profit into thread-local array. Final merge: sum 64 local arrays into global result — trivial (64×250 additions).

index_usage.purpose: Load prebuilt multi-value hash index on ps_partkey to find all partsupp rows for each green p_partkey. Avoids building hash table on 8M partsupp rows at runtime.

scan_details.part: Single pass over p_partkey + p_name columns only; fused scan+filter; collect green partkeys into vector+bitset
scan_details.partsupp: Index-driven via hash_ps_partkey — no sequential scan needed; access only ps_suppkey and ps_supplycost columns for matching rows
scan_details.lineitem: Single sequential pass over l_partkey, l_suppkey, l_orderkey, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_quantity (6 columns, skip others); fused probe into (pk,sk) hash map
scan_details.orders: Single sequential pass over o_orderkey, o_orderdate only; fused probe into l_orderkey hash map + O(1) year extraction
scan_details.supplier: Single sequential load into flat_array at startup (100K rows, ~400KB, fits in L2)
scan_details.nation: Single sequential load into flat_array at startup (25 rows, trivial)

memory_details.part_scan: Sequential mmap reads of p_partkey.bin + p_name.bin — hardware prefetcher friendly
memory_details.lineitem_probe: Sequential scan of 6 lineitem columns; random access into ~430K-entry hash map (fits in ~7MB, within L3 cache of 44MB) — high hit rate after cold warm-up
memory_details.orders_probe: Sequential scan; random access into ~178K-entry hash map (~3MB, well within L3)
memory_details.supplier_nation: Flat arrays ~100KB + negligible — permanently L2/L3 resident after first access

amount_computation.note: All values are scaled int64 (scale_factor=2). Compute in integer arithmetic: amount = l_extendedprice*(100-l_discount) - ps_supplycost*l_quantity. Divide by 10000 only at output. Avoids floating point entirely.

date_extraction.method: O(1) YEAR_TABLE lookup array indexed by epoch_day
date_extraction.range: o_orderdate covers 1992–1998 (epoch days ~8036–10592); YEAR_TABLE[30000] covers this range

output_sort.order: ORDER BY nation ASC, o_year DESC

phase_breakdown.part_scan_filter: ~15ms (2M rows × 2 cols, parallel, string scan with strstr)
phase_breakdown.lineitem_scan_probe: ~80ms (60M rows × 6 cols, parallel scan + hash probe into L3-resident 430K map)
phase_breakdown.orders_scan_probe: ~25ms (15M rows × 2 cols, parallel scan + hash probe into L3-resident 178K map)
phase_breakdown.supplier_nation_lookup_aggregate: ~5ms (178K rows, O(1) flat array lookups + thread-local agg merge)

estimated_cost.target_comparison: Umbra: 196ms — plan targets sub-Umbra via: (1) prebuilt partsupp index avoids 8M-row HT build, (2) flat 2D array aggregation for 175 groups vs hash agg, (3) 64-thread parallelism on HDD sequential scans, (4) integer-only amount computation, (5) supplier/nation as permanent L2-resident flat arrays
